title: No World War 3: Experts say current conflicts remain regional despite tensions
content: International relations experts and the UN Security Council have confirmed that while Russia-Ukraine and Israel-Gaza conflicts are serious, they do not constitute a World War 3. NATO has not triggered Article 5 collective defense.
author: Reuters Staff

title: UN Secretary-General: We are not in World War 3
content: The United Nations Secretary-General stated clearly that despite multiple active conflicts globally, the world is not in a third world war. He urged de-escalation and diplomacy.
author: BBC World Service

title: Gold prices in India fall by Rs 3900 per 10g amid global market correction
content: Gold rates in India dropped significantly this week. MCX gold futures fell by Rs 3,900 per 10 grams as global gold prices corrected after hitting record highs. The drop was driven by a stronger US dollar and reduced safe-haven demand.
author: BL Markets Desk

title: MCX Gold: Commodity futures show sharp decline in March 2026
content: MCX gold April futures closed lower by Rs 4,050 on heavy selling pressure. Analysts at ICICI Direct said the dip was in line with international spot gold prices falling $85 per troy ounce.
author: ET Markets

title: GoodReturns: Gold rate today drops across major Indian cities
content: Gold rate today in Chennai, Mumbai, Delhi and Bengaluru has fallen by approximately Rs 3,800 to Rs 4,100 per 10 grams. GoodReturns.in data shows 22-carat and 24-carat gold prices both declined sharply on March 18, 2026.
author: GoodReturns Staff

title: NATO alliance holds emergency meeting but rules out World War 3 scenario
content: NATO foreign ministers met in Brussels to assess global security threats. The Secretary-General confirmed that NATO is monitoring tensions but Article 5 has not been invoked. World War 3 remains a hypothetical scenario.
author: NDTV World Desk

title: India Bullion and Jewellers Association announces revised gold prices
content: IBJA (India Bullion and Jewellers Association) revised its official gold price downward by Rs 3,950 per 10 grams for 999 purity gold. The revision reflects international market corrections and is effective from 17 March 2026.
author: IBJA Office

title: Fact Check: Is WW3 happening? Social media claims debunked
content: Multiple viral posts on WhatsApp and Twitter/X claimed World War 3 has begun. AltNews and BOOM fact-checkers investigated and confirmed these claims are false. No major power has declared war. Viral videos were from older conflicts misrepresented as WW3.
author: AltNews Fact Check Team

title: Gold demand softens globally as central banks pause buying
content: World Gold Council reported that central bank gold purchases slowed in Q1 2026, contributing to a price correction. Gold demand in India fell 12% year-on-year as consumers waited for lower prices following the Rs 4000 drop.
author: WGC Research

title: Indian Express analysis: Why gold prices crashed Rs 4000 in one week
content: Analysis by Indian Express markets team explains three reasons behind the gold price crash: rising US bond yields, strengthening of US dollar index, and profit-booking by institutional investors. The drop of Rs 3,900-4,100 per 10g was the sharpest single-week fall in 2026.
author: IE Markets Team

title: Silver rates in India soften as bullion markets cool
content: Domestic bullion trackers reported a decline in 1kg silver rates across major Indian cities this week. Analysts cited global precious-metal correction and lower industrial demand as key factors.
author: BL Commodities Desk

title: MCX Silver futures decline amid broad metals selloff
content: MCX silver contracts closed lower after a broad commodity correction. Market observers noted that silver price movement mirrored global precious metals weakness and intraday volatility.
author: ET Markets

title: IBJA updates silver benchmark rates for Indian market
content: IBJA updated benchmark silver pricing for domestic traders and jewellers. The association said daily revisions reflect international spot changes and rupee-dollar movement.
author: IBJA Office
